understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by placing selectable pieces onto every goal tile marked with a '+' at the same time, so that all goals are occupied simultaneously and the engine reports WIN, progressing through all levels to complete the game within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement consisted of selecting a piece (either by direct CLICK on its position or via prior selection) and then attempting to move it; if the destination was blocked by walls/obstacles or otherwise invalid, the piece did not move and a step was still consumed. CLICKing on a coordinate corresponding to a selectable piece changed which piece was active and could also trigger movement/teleport-like repositioning between valid cells when the rules allowed; repeated CLICKs on a blocked target had no effect beyond consuming steps. Success required efficient selection and movement planning because steps were finite per level and a WIN required correct final simultaneous placement, not just moving one piece at a time onto a goal.

history.summary: Level 1 attempt 2/2 failed after 64 turns. No attempts remaining.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to win each level by placing selectable pieces onto every goal tile marked with a '+' at the same time, so that all goals are occupied simultaneously and the engine reports WIN, progressing through all levels to complete the game within the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement consisted of selecting a piece (either by direct CLICK on its position or via prior selection) and then attempting to move it; if the destination was blocked by walls/obstacles or otherwise invalid, the piece did not move and a step was still consumed. CLICKing on a coordinate corresponding to a selectable piece changed which piece was active and could also trigger movement/teleport-like repositioning between valid cells when the rules allowed; repeated CLICKs on a blocked target had no effect beyond consuming steps. Success required efficient selection and movement planning because steps were finite per level and a WIN required correct final simultaneous placement, not just moving one piece at a time onto a goal.
